key_insight: Charged atomic-plane SmNiO3 buffer terminations can deterministically set the polarization direction of ultrathin BaTiO3 and enable net ferroelectric polarization from the first unit cell.
engineering_relevance: This offers a route to reliable, thickness-extreme ferroelectric films for nanoscale memory, sensors, and oxide electronic devices without waiting for a critical thickness.
method: Use (001)-oriented SmNiO3 buffer layers with selectable [SmO]+ or [NiO2]- termination.
Grow BaTiO3 thin films on the polar template to stabilize upward or downward polarization.
Support interpretation with density-functional-theory calculations and defect analysis, especially oxygen vacancies.
evidence_signal: Experiments showed net polarization appears in BaTiO3 from the first unit cell, and DFT helped link the effect to buffer termination and vacancy-induced screening.
watch_item: Polarization control depends on interface termination and defect chemistry, so oxygen vacancies or imperfect buffer preparation may weaken or reverse the intended polarization.
paper_title: Polarizing ultrathin ferroelectric BaTiO3 films through interfacial layer polarization
